ArchitectureNotes.objective: Clean layered architecture: transport adapters depend on shared core, never on each other.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No distributed or multi-node execution patterns.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Backend selection at startup must fail fast with explicit diagnostics when no registered backend is actually ready.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Single inference guard serializes all generation requests across adapters.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Additional Torch-backed families must isolate family semantics from backend loading so OmniVoice does not regress Qwen3 transport behavior.
ArchitectureNotes.migration-objective: Maintain the current multi-platform, multi-backend, multi-model runtime without regressing server, Telegram, or CLI adapter behavior.

migration-invariants.item-1: Current transport adapters continue to depend only on shared core and application contracts.
migration-invariants.item-2: Qwen3-TTS remains the reference family while planner, family-adapter, and backend-contract seams are proven.
migration-invariants.item-3: No big-bang rewrite is allowed; planning and family seams remain explicit and contract-driven.
migration-invariants.item-4: Readiness, observability, and job execution remain first-class runtime artifacts.
migration-invariants.item-5: OmniVoice onboarding reuses the existing Torch backend contract through a family adapter instead of introducing transport-visible forks.

contract.purpose: Parse and validate environment-based runtime configuration for all components.

export-CoreSettings.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all shared runtime settings

export-parse_core_settings_from_env.PURPOSE: Parse environment variables into typed settings dict

contract.purpose: Define typed domain error hierarchy for all core operations.

export-CoreError.PURPOSE: Base exception for all domain errors

export-ModelNotAvailableError.PURPOSE: Raised when requested model cannot be found

export-InferenceBusyError.PURPOSE: Raised when inference slot is occupied

export-JobNotFoundError.PURPOSE: Raised when job ID is not in store

export-RateLimitExceededError.PURPOSE: Raised when rate limit policy is violated

export-QuotaExceededError.PURPOSE: Raised when quota policy is violated

contract.purpose: Provide request context propagation, structured logging, and timing utilities.

export-log_event.PURPOSE: Emit structured JSON log with request context

export-bind_request_context.PURPOSE: Set request ID in context var

export-get_request_id.PURPOSE: Read current request ID from context

contract.purpose: Collect and expose counters, gauges, and timing metrics for runtime operations.
Define command DTOs, job contracts, and result types for the TTS domain.
Register, select, and manage TTS inference backends (MLX, Torch, Qwen Fast, and ONNX).

export-BackendRegistry.PURPOSE: Backend selection and model spec resolution

export-QwenFastBackend.PURPOSE: Accelerated Qwen backend for custom, design, and clone with explicit readiness diagnostics

export-ONNXBackend.PURPOSE: ONNX runtime backend for Piper voices

export-LoadedModelHandle.PURPOSE: Handle to a loaded model with metadata

contract.purpose: Provide local implementations for job execution, audio I/O, admission control, and concurrency.

export-LocalBoundedExecutionManager.PURPOSE: Local async job execution with bounded concurrency

export-convert_audio_to_wav_if_needed.PURPOSE: Audio format normalization for clone flows

export-build_rate_limiter.PURPOSE: Factory for rate limiter from settings

export-build_quota_guard.PURPOSE: Factory for quota guard from settings

M-MODEL-REGISTRY.NAME: ModelRegistry

contract.purpose: Discover, validate, preload, and serve model handles through the selected backend.

export-ModelRegistry.PURPOSE: High-level model discovery and loading facade

M-MODEL-REGISTRY.depends: M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY, M-BACKENDS, M-ERRORS, M-METRICS, M-MODEL-CATALOG, M-MODELS, M-OBSERVABILITY, M-PROFILE-RESOLVER, M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY

contract.purpose: Coordinate inference for custom, design, and clone synthesis modes.

export-TTSService.PURPOSE: Core synthesis coordinator with inference guard

contract.purpose: Provide application-level facade for TTS operations including job execution gateway and admission control.

export-TTSApplicationService.PURPOSE: High-level synthesis facade for transport adapters

M-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: CoreBootstrap

contract.purpose: Assemble the full CoreRuntime from settings by wiring all components together.

export-CoreRuntime.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all wired runtime components

export-build_runtime.PURPOSE: Factory function that assembles CoreRuntime from settings

M-BOOTSTRAP.depends: M-CONFIG, M-BACKENDS, M-MODEL-REGISTRY, M-TTS-SERVICE, M-APPLICATION, M-INFRASTRUCTURE, M-METRICS, M-PROFILES

contract.purpose: Expose TTS capabilities through FastAPI HTTP endpoints with auth, rate limiting, error handling, and API-only transport behavior.
Provide a standalone static one-page demo frontend that targets the existing TTS HTTP API without living inside the server adapter.

export-index-html.PURPOSE: Static page shell for the demo frontend

export-app-js.PURPOSE: Browser-side clone demo flow targeting the HTTP API by configured base URL

export-voice-presets-json.PURPOSE: Local preset configuration owned by the frontend module

contract.purpose: Expose TTS capabilities through Telegram bot commands with allowlist, rate limiting, and delivery persistence.

export-main.PURPOSE: Telegram bot entry point and polling loop

contract.purpose: Provide interactive local CLI for synthesis workflows with optional audio playback.

export-main.PURPOSE: CLI entry point with interactive loop

M-MODELS.NAME: ModelMetadata

contract.purpose: Define the current manifest-backed model metadata, rollout rules, and backend artifact validation surfaces.

export-ModelSpec.PURPOSE: Typed model specification for the current catalog

export-load_model_manifest.PURPOSE: Load and validate manifest metadata from disk

export-get_model_manifest.PURPOSE: Expose the active model manifest to registry consumers

M-MODELS.migration-note: Current model metadata is Qwen3-mode-oriented and will be split into family-aware catalog, artifact, and runtime registry surfaces.

contract.purpose: Define normalized synthesis capability, request, and execution-plan contracts for planned runtime orchestration.

export-SynthesisCapability.PURPOSE: Normalized capability identifier for runtime planning

export-SynthesisRequest.PURPOSE: Normalized request shape consumed by planner and coordinator

export-ExecutionPlan.PURPOSE: Resolved family, model, backend, and execution parameters

contract.purpose: Describe active OS, architecture, runtime dependencies, and accelerator features used by capability-based planning.
Define family-level semantics for capability matching and execution input preparation independent of transport adapters.

export-ModelFamilyAdapter.PURPOSE: Base contract for model-family execution preparation

contract.purpose: Wrap current Qwen3 custom, design, and clone semantics as the first explicit model-family adapter.

export-Qwen3FamilyAdapter.PURPOSE: Family adapter for current Qwen3-TTS execution semantics

contract.purpose: Adapt OmniVoice custom, design, and clone semantics into planner-prepared backend execution inputs.

export-OmniVoiceFamilyAdapter.PURPOSE: Planner-facing family adapter for OmniVoice runtime semantics

M-MODEL-CATALOG.NAME: ModelCatalogRegistry

contract.purpose: Expose family-aware model metadata and stable public identifiers independent of local installation state.

M-MODEL-CATALOG.migration-note: Current code uses this registry as the family-aware metadata surface alongside the runtime-facing registry layers.

M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY.NAME: ArtifactRegistry

contract.purpose: Resolve and validate local model artifacts while distinguishing declared catalog entries from installed assets.

M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY.migration-note: Current code uses this registry as the artifact-state facade over backend inspection for readiness and model-asset validation.

M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY.NAME: RuntimeModelRegistry

contract.purpose: Manage preload policy and runtime cache state independently of declared model metadata and artifact lookup.

M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY.migration-note: Current code exposes runtime-facing preload and cache state through a stable wrapper that reads artifact state and the public ModelRegistry preload contract directly.

contract.purpose: Match requested synthesis capabilities to compatible family, model, backend, and host combinations with explicit rejection reasons.

export-CapabilityResolver.PURPOSE: Compatibility resolver for family, backend, and host selection

M-SYNTHESIS-PLANNER.NAME: SynthesisPlanner

contract.purpose: Resolve normalized synthesis requests into executable plans that preserve transport adapter isolation and treat the registry contract as the single source of model resolution.

export-SynthesisPlanner.PURPOSE: Planner that resolves request capability, family, model, backend, and artifacts

M-SYNTHESIS-PLANNER.migration-note: Current code no longer falls back to direct catalog scanning inside the planner; model resolution is expected to arrive through the registry contract only, with the planning seam typed through core/contracts/runtime.py.

contract.purpose: Own the direct backend execution contract and diagnostics surface used by family-prepared runtime requests.

export-ExecutionRequest.PURPOSE: Backend execution request carrying family-prepared runtime inputs

M-TTS-COORDINATOR.NAME: SynthesisCoordinator

contract.purpose: Turn the current TTS service into a coordinator over normalized requests, planner output, runtime execution, persistence, and observability.

export-TTSService.PURPOSE: Coordinator-shaped synthesis service built on planner and family adapters

M-TTS-COORDINATOR.migration-note: Current code binds planner/coordinator seams to typed runtime registry protocols so service layers depend on explicit contracts instead of concrete registry implementations.

contract.purpose: Define immutable host, family, module, and resolved launch-profile DTOs plus serialization helpers for launcher and operator tooling.

export-HostProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable host capability snapshot used during launch-profile resolution

export-FamilyProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable family profile carrying runtime pack and backend metadata

export-ModuleProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable module profile carrying entrypoint and transport metadata

export-ResolvedLaunchProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable resolved launch profile combining host, family, module, compatibility, backend, and metadata payloads

contract.purpose: Resolve host, family, and module profiles into compatibility-aware launch plans with dependency-pack resolution, expected interpreter calculation, dedicated-env runtime probing, and resolved-profile assembly.

export-ProfileResolver.PURPOSE: Resolver that loads profile catalogs, evaluates compatibility, resolves pack refs, probes dedicated envs, and assembles resolved launch profiles

contract.purpose: Expose the public runtime-profile barrel surface for launcher and operator tooling at the package import boundary.

export-FamilyProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for family profile DTOs

export-HostProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for host profile DTOs

export-ModuleProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for module profile DTOs

export-ProfileResolver.PURPOSE: Public package export for runtime profile resolution

export-ResolvedLaunchProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for resolved launch profile payloads

contract.purpose: Define Docker Desktop deployment assets for the server and Telegram adapters, including compose scenarios and container trust-store wiring.

export-file-server-Dockerfile.PURPOSE: Container image definition for the FastAPI server runtime

export-file-telegram-bot-Dockerfile.PURPOSE: Container image definition for the Telegram bot runtime

export-file-docker-compose-server.PURPOSE: Compose entrypoint for the server deployment contour

export-file-docker-compose-telegram.PURPOSE: Compose entrypoint for the Telegram deployment contour

contract.purpose: Provide the Windows-only interactive PowerShell launcher that resolves profiles, prepares environments, and starts the selected adapter.

export-Main.PURPOSE: Interactive PowerShell launcher entrypoint for guided runtime startup

export-Ensure-FamilyEnvironment.PURPOSE: Bridge family-aware environment creation and readiness checks before launch

export-Ensure-ModelAvailability.PURPOSE: Validate required model assets and trigger guided download flows when missing

contract.purpose: Provide a Windows CMD entrypoint that invokes the interactive PowerShell launcher through an inline command string.

export-launch-windows-cmd.PURPOSE: CMD bridge that executes the PowerShell launcher content via powershell.exe -Command

contract.purpose: Provide the profile-aware launcher CLI for inspect, plan-run, doctor, bootstrap-env, check-env, exec, and create-env runtime contours.

export-parse_args.PURPOSE: Launcher CLI parser for inspect, plan-run, doctor, bootstrap-env, check-env, exec, and create-env commands

export-main.PURPOSE: Launcher command entrypoint for profile inspection, env diagnostics, and execution planning

DF-SYNC-TTS.NAME: Synchronous TTS Generation
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-1: Server receives HTTP request, validates input, applies auth and rate limiting
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-2: Server bootstrap resolves CoreRuntime and calls TTSApplicationService
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-3: TTSApplicationService dispatches to TTSService with the appropriate command
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-4: TTSService acquires inference guard, resolves model via ModelRegistry
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-6: Audio result is read, optionally persisted, and returned as GenerationResult
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-7: Server returns audio bytes in HTTP response
DF-SYNC-TTS.evidence: tts.generation.completed log marker, audio bytes in response

DF-ASYNC-JOB.NAME: Async Job Submission and Retrieval
DF-ASYNC-JOB.step-1: Server receives job submission, validates idempotency key
DF-ASYNC-JOB.step-5: On completion, client retrieves audio via GET /api/v1/tts/jobs/{job_id}/result
DF-ASYNC-JOB.evidence: jobs.submitted counter, jobs.completed counter, job status transitions

DF-CLONE-TTS.NAME: Voice Clone with Reference Audio
DF-CLONE-TTS.TRIGGER: Clone request with reference audio file
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-1: Transport adapter receives reference audio and text
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-2: TTSService copies reference audio to temporary directory
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-4: Backend synthesizes clone with normalized reference
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-5: Temporary files are cleaned up in finally block

DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.NAME: Standalone Demo Frontend Clone Flow
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.TRIGGER: Browser interaction with the root-level frontend_demo module and POST /api/v1/tts/clone through the server API
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-1: Browser loads the standalone static frontend from the `frontend_demo/` module
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-2: Frontend reads local preset metadata from `voice-presets.json`
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-3: User selects a preset and enters synthesis text in the single-page UI
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-4: Frontend loads the selected local reference audio file path from the frontend module
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-5: Frontend checks `/health/ready`, verifies clone support, and blocks synthesis when the active backend is incompatible
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-6: Frontend builds `multipart/form-data` and submits it to `/api/v1/tts/clone` using the configured API base URL
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.step-7: Browser receives WAV audio, creates an object URL, and exposes playback plus download
DF-DEMO-FRONTEND.evidence: Standalone frontend renders, local presets load, clone-capable runtime is surfaced correctly, clone endpoint returns audio, browser exposes playable/downloadable WAV output

DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.NAME: Telegram Bot TTS Flow
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-1: Polling loop receives update from Telegram API
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-2: Dispatcher routes to appropriate handler based on command
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-3: Handler validates user allowlist and rate limit
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-4: Handler creates command DTO and submits to job orchestrator
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-5: Job orchestrator polls for completion and delivers audio via sender
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-6: Delivery metadata is persisted for recovery after restart
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.evidence: Delivery store contains completed job entries

DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.NAME: Backend Autoselection at Startup
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-1: BackendRegistry receives list of backend instances and settings
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-2: If backend is explicitly set, validate that the configured backend is ready before selecting it
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-3: If autoselect is enabled, rank registered backends by capability and host fit, then pick the first ready candidate
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-4: If no registered backend is ready, fail startup with explicit backend diagnostics
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-5: Selected backend loads model manifest and resolves model specs
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-6: ModelRegistry applies preload policy if configured
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.evidence: BackendRegistry selection report, model readiness report

DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.NAME: Planned Request-To-Plan Execution
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.TRIGGER: Application facade receives normalized synthesis command during migration target architecture
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-1: Transport adapter maps incoming payload into application command DTO
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-2: Synthesis coordinator normalizes the request into capability-oriented execution input
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-3: SynthesisPlanner resolves model and backend through the runtime planning contract
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-4: SynthesisCoordinator acquires the backend-specific model handle through the runtime execution contract
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-5: Model-family adapter prepares backend execution input and output semantics
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.step-6: Execution backend performs inference and coordinator persists the result
DF-REQUEST-TO-PLAN.evidence: Planner resolution report, backend execution diagnostics, persisted GenerationResult

DF-STARTUP-READINESS.NAME: Planned Host-Aware Readiness
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.TRIGGER: build_runtime() invocation after migration seams land
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.step-1: HostProbe collects OS, architecture, accelerator, and runtime dependency data
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.step-2: Backend registry enumerates candidates and diagnostics
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.step-3: Model catalog exposes declared models while ArtifactRegistry determines installed assets
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.step-4: CapabilityResolver records why backends and models are accepted or rejected
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.step-5: RuntimeModelRegistry reports preload and cache state for readiness consumers
DF-STARTUP-READINESS.evidence: Readiness payload distinguishes host, backend, catalog, artifact, and runtime states with explicit reasons

DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.NAME: Planned New Family Onboarding
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.TRIGGER: Project adds a second model family after migration seams are stable
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.step-1: New family adapter is registered without changing transport adapter imports
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.step-2: Model catalog and artifact rules are extended for the new family
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.step-3: Planner resolves the new family through existing capability and backend contracts
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.step-4: Readiness and tests prove the family integrates without new Qwen-specific branching in generic modules
DF-NEW-FAMILY-ONBOARDING.evidence: Second-family tests pass and transport adapters remain unchanged

DF-CLI-TTS.NAME: CLI Interactive Synthesis
DF-CLI-TTS.TRIGGER: User enters synthesis command in CLI
DF-CLI-TTS.step-1: CLI runtime reads user input and parses command
DF-CLI-TTS.step-2: CLI creates command DTO from parsed input
DF-CLI-TTS.step-3: TTSApplicationService executes synthesis via core runtime
DF-CLI-TTS.step-4: Audio is optionally saved and/or played via sounddevice

Phase-1.name: Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Core utilities and configuration without external dependencies.
Phase-1.step-5: Command DTOs, job contracts, result types

Phase-2.name: Core Services
Phase-2.goal: Backend selection, model management, and inference coordination.
Phase-2.step-1: Backend registry with MLX and Torch
Phase-2.step-2: Local job execution, audio I/O, admission control

Phase-3.name: Runtime Assembly
Phase-3.goal: Wire all core components into a single runtime.

Phase-4.name: Transport Adapters
Phase-4.goal: Expose TTS through HTTP, Telegram, and CLI.

Migration-Phase-0.name: Baseline Freeze
Migration-Phase-0.goal: Lock the migration baseline in GRACE XML artifacts before code architecture changes begin.
Migration-Phase-0.step-1: Update development, knowledge-graph, and verification artifacts with target modules, flows, and phase gates.

Migration-Phase-1.name: Planning and Family Seams
Migration-Phase-1.goal: Introduce normalized synthesis contracts, planner seam, and model-family base contract without changing runtime behavior.
Migration-Phase-1.step-1: Define normalized request, capability, and execution-plan DTOs.
Migration-Phase-1.step-2: Add planner seam and initial compatibility implementation.

Migration-Phase-2.name: Qwen3 Family Extraction
Migration-Phase-2.goal: Wrap current Qwen3 semantics in an explicit family adapter.
Migration-Phase-2.step-1: Move current custom, design, and clone semantics into a Qwen3 family adapter.

Migration-Phase-3.name: Coordinator Refactor
Migration-Phase-3.goal: Turn the current TTS service into a planner-driven synthesis coordinator.
Migration-Phase-3.step-1: Refactor TTSService into a coordinator over planning, execution, and persistence.

Migration-Phase-4.name: Catalog and Manifest Evolution
Migration-Phase-4.goal: Move from Qwen3-mode-only model descriptors to family-aware model metadata while preserving v1 compatibility.
Migration-Phase-4.step-1: Add family-aware model catalog metadata and stable public model IDs.

Migration-Phase-5.name: Registry Split
Migration-Phase-5.goal: Separate declared model metadata, local artifact state, and runtime-loaded model handles.
Migration-Phase-5.step-1: Add artifact registry for install and validation state.
Migration-Phase-5.step-2: Add runtime model registry for preload and cache ownership.

Migration-Phase-6.name: Backend Contract Evolution
Migration-Phase-6.goal: Replace Qwen3-shaped backend calls with runtime-oriented execution contracts.
Migration-Phase-6.step-1: Generalize backend load, inspect, execute, and diagnostics surfaces.

Migration-Phase-7.name: Host-Aware Planning
Migration-Phase-7.goal: Replace platform-order backend choice with explainable capability-based selection.
Migration-Phase-7.step-2: Add explicit compatibility resolution and rejection reasons.
Migration-Phase-7.step-3: Define host, family, module, and resolved launch profile DTOs for operator-facing planning payloads.
Migration-Phase-7.step-4: Resolve compatibility, dependency packs, expected interpreters, and dedicated-env readiness into launch profiles.

Migration-Phase-8.name: Second Family Proof
Migration-Phase-8.goal: Prove the architecture with one explicitly selected non-Qwen model family.
Migration-Phase-8.step-1: Lock the second family in the execution packet before implementation.
Migration-Phase-8.step-2: Integrate the second family without transport adapter rewrites.

Migration-Phase-9.name: Platform Hardening
Migration-Phase-9.goal: Make multi-platform support operational with diagnostics, packaging guidance, and smoke evidence.
Migration-Phase-9.step-1: Document support matrix and runtime dependency paths per platform.
Migration-Phase-9.step-2: Extend readiness and smoke verification surfaces for supported hosts.
Migration-Phase-9.step-3: Publish the launcher-consumed profile surface through a package barrel instead of direct resolver imports.
Migration-Phase-9.step-4: Expose inspect, env guidance, execution planning, and profile-aware launch commands through the shared profiles package boundary.

Migration-Phase-10.name: Legacy Cleanup
Migration-Phase-10.goal: Remove transitional seams only after the new architecture is proven by verification evidence.
Migration-Phase-10.step-1: Deprecate obsolete internal paths and finalize stable module ownership.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
